description_long: This is a bright red biplane with two stacked wings, each featuring black and white cross insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the tail, with a single propeller mounted at the nose. The cockpit is open and positioned just behind the upper wing, suggesting a single-seat configuration. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted red with matching insignias. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, typical of vintage taildragger designs. The surface appears metallic with a slightly weathered, glossy finish, and the structure is supported by visible struts between the wings.
description: A red vintage biplane with black and white cross insignias, open cockpit, and dual stacked wings supported by struts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and symmetrical cross insignias on the wings.
description_view_1: Angled top-left view highlighting the open cockpit, wing struts, and tail insignia.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the alignment of the wings.
description_view_3: Angled top-right view emphasizing the cockpit position, wing structure, and propeller detail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit positioned just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
